heading: Medal of honor; Air Force cross; distinguished-service medal: limitations on award
label: 9274 Medal of honor; Air Force cross; distinguished-service medal: limitations on award
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)8744(a)8744(b)8744(c)10:1411.10:1409 (words before 1st semicolon).10:1409 (words after 2d semicolon).(12th par., less words after 2d semicolon, under “Ordnance Department”); restated (less last sentence), .July 9, 1918, ch. 143Jan. 24, 1920, ch. 55, § 141 Stat. 398(less words between 1st and 2d semicolons of 15th par. under “Ordnance Department”), .July 9, 1918, ch. 14340 Stat. 871In subsection (a), the words “may be awarded to a person” are substituted for the words “shall be issued to any one person” to conform to the other subsections of the revised section.In subsection (b), the word “thereof” is substituted for the words “of either of said medal or of said cross”. The words “Except as otherwise prescribed in this section”, “at the time of”, “specific”, “official”, and “has so distinguished himself as” are omitted as surplusage.In subsection (c), 10:1409 (words after 3d semicolon) is omitted as executed. The words “hereinbefore authorized” are omitted as surplusage.

Amendments2019—Subsec. (b). , substituted “subsection (c)” for “subsection (d)” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 116–92, § 582(b)(3)(A)Subsecs. (c), (d). , (C), redesignated subsec. (d) as (c) and struck out former subsec. (c) which read as follows: “No medal of honor, Air Force cross, distinguished-service medal, or device in place thereof, may be awarded or presented to a person whose service after he distinguished himself has not been honorable.”Pub. L. 116–92, § 582(b)(3)(B)2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 8744 of this title2013—Subsec. (a). , substituted “Air Force Cross” for “medal of honor, Air Force cross,”.Pub. L. 113–66, § 561(c)Subsec. (b)(1). , substituted “five years” for “three years”.Pub. L. 113–66, § 562(b)(1)(A)Subsec. (b)(2). , substituted “three years” for “two years”.Pub. L. 113–66, § 562(b)(1)(B)Subsec. (d)(1). , substituted “three years” for “two years”.Pub. L. 113–66, § 562(b)(2)1960— substituted “Air Force cross” for “distinguished-service cross” in section catchline and wherever appearing in subsecs. (a) to (d).Pub. L. 86–593Subsec. (b). , substituted “Except as provided in subsection (d), no” for “No”.Pub. L. 86–582, § 1(3)(A)Subsec. (d). , added subsec. (d).Pub. L. 86–582, § 1(3)(B)

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019

Persons Awarded Distinguished-Service Cross or Soldier’s Medal BeforeJuly 6, 1960, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 86–593, § 274 Stat. 332Pub. L. 115–232, div. A, title VIII, § 809(b)(12)132 Stat. 1841July 6, 1960Aug. 13, 2018“For the purposes of sections 9274(a) and 9280(b) of title 10, United States Code, a person who was awarded a distinguished-service cross or Soldier’s Medal before the date of enactment of this Act [] shall be treated as if he had not been awarded an Air Force cross or Airman’s Medal, as the case may be.”July 6, 1960

Persons Awarded Distinguished-Service Cross or Soldier’s Medal BeforeJuly 6, 1960, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 86–593, § 274 Stat. 332Pub. L. 115–232, div. A, title VIII, § 809(b)(12)132 Stat. 1841July 6, 1960Aug. 13, 2018“For the purposes of sections 9274(a) and 9280(b) of title 10, United States Code, a person who was awarded a distinguished-service cross or Soldier’s Medal before the date of enactment of this Act [] shall be treated as if he had not been awarded an Air Force cross or Airman’s Medal, as the case may be.”July 6, 1960

children.label: a
children.text: No more than one Air Force Cross or distinguished-service medal may be awarded to a person. However, for each succeeding act that would otherwise justify the award of such a medal or cross, the President may award a suitable bar or other device to be worn as he directs.
the award is made within five years after the date of the act justifying the award;
a statement setting forth the distinguished service and recommending official recognition of it was made within three years after the distinguished service; and
it appears from records of the Department of the Air Force that the person is entitled to the award.
a statement setting forth the distinguished service and recommending official recognition of it was made and supported by sufficient evidence within three years after the distinguished service; and
no award was made, because the statement was lost or through inadvertence the recommendation was not acted on;
